title: Multi-Language Prompt Management
subtitle: Multi-Language Prompt Management
description: Prompts in Agent systems are scattered across modules and require unified management with multi-language support, hot updates, and version control. This domain addresses how to decouple prompts from code and implement structured prompt asset management.
sub_problems: Decoupling prompts from code via YAML/JSON externalization
Organizing prompt files by module/agent hierarchy
Secure prompt access and missing value fallback mechanisms
Nested subdirectory prompt path resolution using recursive discovery
Automated prompt injection integration at BaseAgent layer
best_practices: Externalize prompts using YAML files organized in three-level directories: module/agent/language
Implement language fallback chains to gracefully degrade to default language when translations are missing
Use Singleton pattern with class-level caching to avoid redundant I/O and support module-granularity cache invalidation
Inject prompts uniformly in BaseAgent constructor so subclasses don't need manual invocation
Standardize multiple language input formats with parse_language() into internal language codes

values.DeepTutor: YAML files with multi-line strings (|) and flat key-value structure
module/prompts/lang/[subdir/]agent.yaml with three levels plus optional subdirectories
Hardcoded fallback chain zh→cn→en, unknown languages default to en
Class-level dictionary cache with Singleton pattern, supporting module-level invalidation and forced reload
Automatic injection in BaseAgent constructor, unified management of 50+ YAML files across 6 modules
Three-tier strategy: subdirectory priority → direct path → recursive search

solutions.title: Multi-Language Prompt Management
solutions.description: Unified management of loading, caching, degradation, and organizational structure for multi-language prompts across Agent systems
